title: Sethia magnoliifolia
taxonomy: Sethia magnoliifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to South Asia and is a part of the Rutaceae family, which also includes citrus and rue. The Rutaceae family is a part of the larger order Sapindales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The exact classification of Sethia magnoliifolia is still being studied and confirmed by botanists.

intro: Sethia magnoliifolia, also known as S. mukul, is a plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae, commonly used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Sethia magnoliifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to help manage various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include cardol and cardanol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
